meta.title: 5.4 Checkpoint
meta.description: Put it all together: review AI-generated landing page code, find structural issues, visual bugs, JS errors, and accessibility problems, then write prompts to fix them.

blocks.question: Before looking at the questions, how many issues do you think you spotted in the code above? Write down your count.
blocks.answer: There are at least 7 distinct issues in this code spanning HTML structure, CSS bugs, JavaScript errors, and accessibility problems. If you found 4 or more on your own, your review skills are strong. If you found fewer, the practice problems below will help you build that eye.

blocks.question: Which category do you think has the most issues: HTML structure, CSS styling, JavaScript logic, or accessibility?
blocks.answer: Accessibility has the most issues (div-as-button, missing alt text, div-as-nav, color contrast). This is realistic — AI-generated code most frequently falls short on accessibility, which is exactly why knowing what to check for matters so much.

problems.question: The hero section text is invisible when you load the page. Look at the `.hero` CSS. What is the problem?
options: A) The `text-align: center` is pushing the text off screen
B) The `color` and `background-color` are both set to `#16213e`, making text invisible against the background
C) The `padding: 100px 20px` is too large and hides the text below the fold
problems.explanation: The .hero rule sets both background-color and color to #16213e (a dark blue). The text is there but it is the same color as the background — invisible. The fix: change color to #ffffff or another light color that contrasts with the dark background.

problems.question: The media query is supposed to make the page work on mobile, but the nav links disappear on desktop and the card grid becomes single-column on desktop. What is wrong?
options: A) The media query uses `min-width: 768px` when it should use `max-width: 768px`
B) The `.nav-links` class name does not match the HTML
problems.explanation: The media query says @media (min-width: 768px), which means it applies on screens 768px and wider — that is tablets and desktops. The mobile-specific rules (hiding nav links, single-column grid) are being applied to large screens instead. Changing to max-width: 768px fixes this.

problems.question: The hamburger menu toggle does not work — clicking it does nothing. Look at the JavaScript `toggleMenu()` function and the HTML. What is the bug?
options: A) The `onclick` attribute should be `onClick` with a capital C
B) The JavaScript targets `.nav-list` but the HTML uses the class `.nav-links`
C) The `classList.toggle` method does not exist in JavaScript
problems.explanation: The JavaScript uses document.querySelector('.nav-list') but the HTML element has class="nav-links". The selector does not match any element, so querySelector returns null and the toggle fails silently (or throws a console error). The fix: change '.nav-list' to '.nav-links' in the JavaScript.

problems.question:
The "Start Free Trial" call-to-action is built like this: ```html
Start Free Trial
``` What is the accessibility problem?
options: A) The `onclick` handler should use `addEventListener` instead
B) The `<div>` should be a `<button>` so it is keyboard-accessible and announced as a button by screen readers
C) The class name `cta-btn` is misleading because it is not a real button
problems.explanation: A <div> is not focusable with the Tab key, not announced as a button by screen readers, and does not respond to Enter or Space key presses. Using <button class="cta-btn" onclick="signup()"> gives you all of that accessibility for free. This is the same issue from Module 5.2 — it is that common.

problems.question: All three class card images are missing something important: ```html ``` What is missing and why does it matter?
options: A) They are missing `width` and `height` attributes, which causes layout shift
B) They are missing `alt` attributes, which means screen reader users get no description of the images
C) They are missing `loading="lazy"` which hurts page performance
problems.explanation: Every should have an alt attribute with a meaningful description, such as alt="Woman in a yoga pose on a mat". Without alt text, screen reader users hear nothing or just the file name. This is an accessibility requirement and one of the most common omissions in AI-generated code.

problems.question:
The navigation is built with `<div class="nav">` and the footer uses `<div class="footer">`. The page logo also uses an `<h1>` tag inside the nav. Which prompt would fix the structural and semantic issues?

**Prompt A:** "Change the navigation `<div class="nav">` to a `<nav>` element, change the footer `<div class="footer">` to a `<footer>` element, and change the logo `<h1>` in the nav to a `<span>` or `<a>` since the page should have only one `<h1>` (which is already in the hero section)."

**Prompt B:** "Make the HTML more semantic."

**Prompt C:** "Add ARIA roles to the div elements."
problems.explanation: Prompt A identifies three specific issues (div instead of nav, div instead of footer, duplicate h1) and gives the exact fix for each. Prompt B is too vague — the AI might change some things but miss others. Prompt C is a workaround; using the correct semantic elements is better than adding ARIA roles to generic divs.

blocks.title: Write a single follow-up prompt to the AI that addresses ALL of the issues found in this checkpoint. Be specific: name every element, property, and behavior that needs to change. Organize your prompt so the AI can work through the fixes systematically.
blocks.solution:
A strong follow-up prompt would cover all the issues found: "Fix the following issues in the FitStudio landing page: (1) In the .hero CSS, change color from #16213e to #ffffff so the text is visible against the dark background. (2) Change the media query from min-width: 768px to max-width: 768px so mobile styles only apply on small screens. (3) In the toggleMenu() JavaScript function, change the selector from '.nav-list' to '.nav-links' to match the HTML class. (4) Change the
to a
